items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View approved a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading of its development agreement. This project will add housing to an existing commercial corridor in Mountain View.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, which provides funding to communities that remove barriers to housing development. This could bring state dollars to help increase local housing supply.

items.headline: City seeking public input on $HUD housing grants for next year
items.summary: Santa Clara is opening a comment period on how to spend federal housing funds for fiscal year 2026/27. Residents can weigh in on priorities for affordable housing and community development programs at the March 24 meeting.

items.headline: City holds community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program likely addresses vehicle dwelling and parking access for unhoused residents in the city.

items.headline: Task force reviewing station area land use and design plans
items.summary: The Station Area Task Force is examining the land use plan and design guidelines for the area around Santa Clara Station. These guidelines will shape what buildings, housing, and development can happen in this key transit neighborhood.
